user_query:
I want to include a short, reproducible appendix in my keynote that shows my inclusive tech setup on this machine. Please use the included probe script to capture a local system snapshot and then summarize it for my slides.

Requirements:
1) Run the provided probe and capture its complete output to a log so we can audit later:
   - Run: bash scripts/probe.sh and save both stdout and stderr to reports/probe_raw.log (create the reports directory if needed).
2) Based on reports/probe_raw.log, generate a concise summary at reports/system_inclusion_report.txt with exactly these lines and labels in this order:
   - OS: <best available OS identifier>
   - Default LANG: <value or Unknown>
   - Locale availability: en_US.UTF-8=<Found|Not Found|Unknown>, es_ES.UTF-8=<Found|Not Found|Unknown>
   - Text-to-Speech tool (espeak-ng): <Present|Absent|Unknown>
   - Captioning tool (ffmpeg): <Present|Absent|Unknown>
   - Noto Sans font: <Present|Absent|Unknown>
   - Probe timestamp: <timestamp from the log>

Classification rules you must follow using the content in reports/probe_raw.log:
- OS: Use the OS_PRETTY_NAME value from the OS section if present; otherwise use the UNAME line.
- Default LANG: In the LOCALE section, prefer the LANG_ENV value if present; otherwise the LANG= value; if the LOCALE section contains an ERROR line, use Unknown.
- Locale availability: Inspect the LOCALE_LIST section. If it contains an ERROR line, mark both locales Unknown. Otherwise, "Found" if the exact line en_US.UTF-8 or es_ES.UTF-8 appears; "Not Found" if the section is present but the locale line is absent.
- Text-to-Speech tool (espeak-ng): In the TTS section, "Present" if the line starts with "espeak-ng: " followed by a filesystem path, "Absent" if it ends with "NOT FOUND", and "Unknown" only if the section shows an ERROR.
- Captioning tool (ffmpeg): In the CAPTION section, same logic as TTS but for ffmpeg.
- Noto Sans font: In the FONTS section, "Present" if any line contains the string "Noto Sans" (case-sensitive), "Absent" if the section exists and no such line appears, and "Unknown" if the section shows an ERROR.
- Probe timestamp: Use the value from the line starting with "Timestamp:".

3) Update docs/keynote_outline.md by replacing only the content between the markers <!-- APPENDIX_TECH_ENV_START --> and <!-- APPENDIX_TECH_ENV_END --> with a short bullet list that mirrors the seven items from reports/system_inclusion_report.txt (same order and labels), so it is ready to paste into my slides.

Constraints:
- Do not invent information. Every status must be derived from reports/probe_raw.log and follow the classification rules above.
- The two deliverables must exist and be consistent: reports/probe_raw.log (raw capture) and reports/system_inclusion_report.txt (summary). The updated docs/keynote_outline.md must reflect the same statuses.
- Keep everything local; do not use the internet.

Input paths:
- scripts/probe.sh
- docs/keynote_outline.md

Output paths:
- reports/probe_raw.log
- reports/system_inclusion_report.txt
- docs/keynote_outline.md (modified in place) All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
